name: Kagawa Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the olive groves of Shodoshima, an emblem of the prefecture, and the lushness of Kagawa
peace, purity and the bright future of the prefecture

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white emblem of stylised olive leaves arranged to suggest the kanji 香 of the prefecture's name.
history: Kagawa's prefectural symbol was adopted on 11 May 1977. The flag, with the white emblem on a green field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the olive groves of Shodoshima and the verdant landscape of Kagawa

features.role: Kagawa prefectural emblem (olive leaves stylised as kanji 香)
features.symbolism: a stylised arrangement of olive leaves shaped to evoke the kanji 香 (ka) of 香川 (Kagawa). Olives have been Kagawa's prefectural symbol since they were first introduced to Japan on Shodoshima island in 1908; the leaves represent peace, prosperity and harmony.
